user_story: As an anonymous user, I want to create an account with email and password so that I can access the NBHD United platform.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/auth/signup/ accepts {email, password, display_name?, invite_code?}. Email and password are required (400 if missing). If PREVIEW_ACCESS_KEY is set, invite_code must match exactly (403 otherwise). Duplicate email returns 409. On success, creates User with username=email, hashes password, sets display_name; returns {access, refresh} JWT pair with 201. No Tenant is created here — onboarding is a separate step. Password is hashed by Django's create_user; password_last_changed_at is NOT set at signup (only set by set_password/set_unusable_password).
inventory_notes: RefreshToken.for_user is used here (not EmailTokenObtainPairSerializer), so the access token does NOT carry the pw_iat claim. This means JWTAuthenticationWithRLS will accept it even for users with a non-null password_last_changed_at, because pw_iat=0 is only rejected when password_last_changed_at is non-null.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to log in with my email and password so that I get a JWT pair to authenticate subsequent requests.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/auth/login/ delegates to TokenObtainPairView backed by EmailTokenObtainPairSerializer. Accepts {email, password}. Authenticates against email (not username). Returns {access, refresh}. The access token carries a pw_iat claim (unix timestamp of user.password_last_changed_at, or 0). 401 if credentials invalid or user inactive. The serializer overrides the default username_field to 'email'.
inventory_notes: pw_iat=0 when the user has never had a password rotation. JWTAuthenticationWithRLS only rejects if token pw_iat < current password_last_changed_at — legacy users (pw_iat=0, password_last_changed_at=null) are always accepted.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to refresh my access token so that I stay logged in without re-entering credentials.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/auth/refresh/ is the stock simplejwt TokenRefreshView. Accepts {refresh} and returns {access}. Refresh tokens are blacklisted on logout. Force-logout: if the access token's pw_iat < password_last_changed_at, the new access token will also fail on next use because the middleware rechecks on every authenticated request.
inventory_notes: Refresh tokens are blacklisted by LogoutView; using a blacklisted refresh token returns 401.

feature: JWT-authenticated request with RLS context + pw_iat force-logout
user_story: As a subscriber, I want every authenticated API call to enforce my session freshness and tenant isolation so that my data is secure.
expected_behaviour: JWTAuthenticationWithRLS wraps simplejwt. On every request: decodes Bearer token, loads User+Tenant via select_related, checks user.is_active, then compares token pw_iat against user.password_last_changed_at. If pw_iat < password_last_changed_at, raises 401 with code=password_rotated. On success, sets thread-local tenant context and Postgres session vars (app.tenant_id, app.user_id) in a single round-trip via set_rls_context(). TenantContextMiddleware clears context on response. UserTimezoneMiddleware activates user's IANA timezone for each request.
inventory_notes: RLS session vars are set in a single SELECT to avoid multiple cross-region round trips. reset_rls_context() skips the DB call if rls_dirty flag is False — optimization for unauthenticated 404s.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to log out so that my refresh token is invalidated and my session ends.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/auth/logout/ requires IsAuthenticated. Accepts {refresh}; 400 if missing. Calls RefreshToken(refresh_token).blacklist() via simplejwt token blacklist. Returns 204 on success. Invalid token returns 400.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to retrieve my user and tenant details in one call so that the dashboard can render my account state.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/auth/me/ requires IsAuthenticated. Returns UserSerializer data (id, username, email, display_name, language, timezone, telegram_chat_id, telegram_username, line_user_id, line_display_name, preferred_channel, location fields) plus nested TenantSerializer under 'tenant' key. Tenant may be null if onboarding not done. Response is cached 60s per user via @tenant_cache(ttl=60, tag='tenant').

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to read my tenant record so that I can see my plan, usage, and container status.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/tenants/ and GET /api/v1/tenants/<id>/ via ReadOnlyModelViewSet. Scoped to request.user.tenant only — cross-tenant access is impossible. GET /api/v1/tenants/me/ action also available (cached 60s). TenantSerializer includes computed fields: has_active_subscription, trial_days_remaining, platform_budget_exceeded (reads MonthlyBudget), gravity_available, effective_model, free_model_offer. All fields are read-only. 404 if user has no tenant.
inventory_notes: TenantSerializer exposes finance_enabled, fuel_enabled, core_enabled, byo_models_enabled as read-only flags. Gravity availability is gated by GRAVITY_ENABLED env var regardless of stored flag.

user_story: As a subscriber completing signup, I want to submit my profile details so that a tenant and OpenClaw container are created and provisioning begins.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/tenants/onboard/ requires IsAuthenticated. Accepts {display_name, language, timezone, agent_persona}; all optional with defaults. Timezone is validated against IANA database. agent_persona must be in PERSONAS dict. If user already has a tenant in PENDING/PROVISIONING status, returns 409. If tenant exists in any other state, also 409. On success: saves display_name/language/timezone/agent_persona to User; creates Tenant with is_trial=True, trial_started_at=now, trial_ends_at=max(now+7d, 2026-03-31T23:59:59Z), status=PROVISIONING, model_tier=STARTER; calls seed_default_templates_for_tenant; publishes 'provision_tenant' QStash task. If QStash publish fails, status reverts to PENDING and 503 is returned. Returns TenantSerializer(tenant).data with 201.
inventory_notes: Trial end date has a hardcoded March 2026 promotion floor — comment says 'TODO: revert to timedelta(days=7)'. This is stale code that will give every new user a trial extending to at least March 2026.

user_story: As a subscriber waiting for my assistant to be set up, I want to poll provisioning status so that the UI can show readiness.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/tenants/provisioning-status/ requires IsAuthenticated. Returns {tenant_id, user_id, status, container_id, container_fqdn, has_container_id, has_container_fqdn, provisioned_at, created_at, updated_at, ready}. ready=true only when status=active AND container_id AND container_fqdn are all set. 404 if no tenant.
inventory_notes: No caching — intended to be polled during provisioning flow.

user_story: As a subscriber whose provisioning got stuck, I want to retry it so that setup can complete.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/tenants/retry-provisioning/ requires IsAuthenticated. 404 if no tenant. Returns 200 if already active+ready. Returns 400 if status is SUSPENDED/DEPROVISIONING/DELETED. Enforces 90-second cooldown: if status=PROVISIONING and updated_at < 90s ago, returns 429 with retry_after_seconds. Otherwise sets status=PROVISIONING and publishes 'provision_tenant' QStash task; 202 on success. 503 if QStash publish fails (reverts to PENDING).
inventory_notes: Cooldown is based on updated_at not on a dedicated retry counter, so any save to the tenant in the past 90s will artificially extend it.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to read and update my profile (timezone, location, language, preferred_channel) so that the assistant knows my context.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/tenants/profile/ returns UserSerializer data (all profile fields, read-only: id, username, email, telegram/line IDs). PATCH /api/v1/tenants/profile/ accepts any subset of writable fields: display_name, language, timezone (IANA validation), preferred_channel, location_city, location_lat (-90 to 90), location_lon (-180 to 180). On timezone change: bumps pending_config_version, calls update_tenant_config (or defers if hibernated), then iterates all cron.list jobs and calls cron.update to sync the tz field on each. On location change: bumps pending_config_version and calls update_tenant_config. Returns {…fields, applied: 'ok'|'pending'}.
inventory_notes: Timezone sync iterates ALL existing cron jobs and patches each one — this is a live gateway call, not a config regen. If the container is hibernated, apply_or_defer_gateway_call defers all calls and applied='pending'.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see available agent personas so that I can choose one during onboarding or settings.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/tenants/personas/ requires IsAuthenticated. Calls list_personas() from apps/orchestrator/personas. Response cached 300s per user via @tenant_cache. Returns list of persona objects.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to change my agent's persona so that the assistant's tone matches my preference.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/tenants/preferences/ returns {agent_persona} from user.preferences (cached 120s). PATCH /api/v1/tenants/preferences/ accepts {agent_persona}. Validates against PERSONAS dict (400 for unknown). Saves to user.preferences JSONField. If tenant is ACTIVE, bumps pending_config_version. Returns {agent_persona}.
inventory_notes: Only bumps pending config — does NOT trigger immediate update_tenant_config call unlike profile changes.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to manually refresh my assistant's configuration so that setting changes apply immediately without waiting for the next cron cycle.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/tenants/refresh-config/ returns {can_refresh, last_refreshed, cooldown_seconds=300, status, has_pending_update, container_image_tag, latest_image_tag, image_outdated}. POST /api/v1/tenants/refresh-config/ requires tenant.status=ACTIVE (409 if PENDING/PROVISIONING, 400 otherwise). Enforces 5-minute cooldown on config_refreshed_at (429 if too soon). Calls update_tenant_config(tenant_id) from orchestrator; on success sets config_refreshed_at=now and config_version=pending_config_version. Returns {detail, last_refreshed}. 502 if update_tenant_config raises.
dependencies: apps/orchestrator (update_tenant_config), Azure Container Apps (file share write), OpenClaw container
inventory_notes: image_outdated is True when OPENCLAW_IMAGE_TAG setting != tenant.container_image_tag and neither is 'latest'.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to configure my assistant's daily check-in window and feature tips so that proactive messages happen at convenient times.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/tenants/heartbeat/ returns {enabled, start_hour, window_hours, feature_tips}. PATCH /api/v1/tenants/heartbeat/ accepts {enabled (bool), start_hour (0-23), window_hours (1-6, max 6), feature_tips (bool)}; all optional. Saves changed fields, runs full_clean() (validates heartbeat_window_hours <= 6). If tenant is ACTIVE: bumps pending_config_version, calls apply_or_defer_gateway_call(update_tenant_config). If enabled or start_hour changed: also calls apply_or_defer_gateway_call(sync_heartbeat_cron). Returns {enabled, start_hour, window_hours, feature_tips, applied: 'ok'|'pending'}.
inventory_notes: window_hours validated at both serializer level (max_value=6) and model clean(). Not writing window_hours via PATCH is intentional — only enabled, start_hour, and feature_tips are settable here.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to delete my account so that my data is removed from the platform.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/tenants/delete-account/ requires IsAuthenticated. Body must contain {"confirm": "DELETE"} (400 otherwise). Idempotent: if pending_deletion already True, returns 200 with existing schedule. With active Stripe subscription: calls stripe.Subscription.modify(cancel_at_period_end=True), sets pending_deletion=True and deletion_scheduled_at=period_end. Stale subscription IDs are cleared. Without active subscription: immediately calls _do_hard_delete() which deprovisions the Azure container and calls user.delete(). Returns {scheduled, deletion_scheduled_at, detail}.
inventory_notes: Hard-delete triggered by webhook (customer.subscription.deleted) for subscription users; immediate for free/trial users. Deprovision failure during hard-delete is logged but not fatal — user record is still deleted.

user_story: As a subscriber who requested deletion but changed my mind, I want to cancel the deletion so that my account remains active.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/tenants/cancel-deletion/ requires IsAuthenticated. Returns 400 if no pending_deletion. If stripe_subscription_id is set: calls stripe.Subscription.modify(cancel_at_period_end=False). Stale subscription IDs are cleared silently. Sets pending_deletion=False, deletion_scheduled_at=None. Returns 200 with {detail: 'Deletion cancelled.'}.
inventory_notes: Stripe errors that are not 'missing subscription' are logged as warnings but do not block the DB update — the deletion is cancelled even if Stripe call fails.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to switch my assistant's primary AI model so that I can use a different model within my tier.
expected_behaviour: PATCH /api/v1/tenants/settings/preferred-model/ requires IsAuthenticated. Body: {preferred_model: string or ''}. Validates model_id against _get_allowed_models(tenant) which combines tier base models + live free offer + BYO extras (400 if not in set). Empty string clears to tier default. Saves preferred_model, calls bump_pending_config(), calls _enqueue_immediate_apply() (publishes 'apply_single_tenant_config' QStash task with idempotency key, skipped if hibernated). Returns {preferred_model, model_tier}.
inventory_notes: Immediate apply enqueue is fire-and-forget — failure is logged but not raised. The hourly cron is the safety net.
